behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is row number loss or DLQ write failure during validation. A unit test of validation logic alone won't catch row number propagation failures. Integration tests the validation → DLQ pipeline together, verifying row numbers are preserved and rows actually reach the queue.
behaviors.test_description: Create a CSV with rows missing required fields, process through the full pipeline, verify the dead-letter queue contains those rows with correct original row numbers preserved

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format
behaviors.justification: Pure business logic with no I/O, database, or state dependencies. The failure modes (unrecognized format, wrong conversion, edge cases like leap years) are all deterministic and testable without external systems.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized unit tests covering ISO 8601, US (MM/DD/YYYY), and European (DD/MM/YYYY) date formats with edge cases (leap years, century boundaries), verify conversion to canonical format

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory
behaviors.justification: Real failures here are OOM or timeout. Unit tests on small samples won't trigger memory exhaustion. Integration with a local 500k-row file might work, but the pipeline reads from S3, so memory overhead from S3 client buffering, real I/O latency, and actual OS timeout behavior must be tested. Mocking I/O hides the real bottleneck.
behaviors.test_description: Generate or retrieve a 500k-row CSV from S3 (or S3-compatible mock), measure memory usage and execution time, verify pipeline completes within configured timeout without OOM errors

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch
behaviors.justification: The real failure is S3-specific: actual SDK retry behavior, network timeout configuration, and timing under real conditions. A mock that returns 503 on call 1-2, success on call 3 tests our retry wrapper, but misses actual S3 SDK behavior (some SDKs have built-in retries that conflict with custom logic), real backoff timing under network latency, and production configuration differences.
behaviors.test_description: Use S3-compatible service (MinIO, LocalStack) configured to return 503 for first N requests, verify pipeline retries the exact expected number of times with exponential backoff timing, and batch processing continues without total failure

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch
behaviors.justification: Idempotency failure requires testing checkpoint logic and PostgreSQL transaction/constraint semantics together. Unit tests won't catch database-level failures (constraints not enforced, transaction isolation issues). Integration with a real test PostgreSQL and actual checkpoint mechanism catches the coordination failure between state tracking and database writes.
behaviors.test_description: Spin up test PostgreSQL, run pipeline to write N rows with checkpoint at row M, kill and restart pipeline, verify no rows 1-N are duplicated in the database
